title: Irans Oil Network Matures Under Sanctions Pressure
section_path: Irans Oil Network Matures Under Sanctions Pressure
section_path_text: Irans Oil Network Matures Under Sanctions Pressure
summary:
Main
Iran's shadow fleet has matured, sustaining 1.3-1.6 mbd exports despite UN snapback sanctions, with faster, more efficient STS operations to China. By Claire Jungman
When the UN's snapback sanctions on Iran officially returned on September 27, many expected exports to falter. Instead, tankers kept loading, ships kept sailing and China - Iran's top buyer - kept taking cargoes.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Iran's shadow fleet has matured, sustaining 1.3-1.6 mbd exports despite UN snapback sanctions, with faster, more efficient STS operations to China. By Claire Jungman
When the UN's snapback sanctions on Iran officially returned on September 27, many expected exports to falter.

children.title: What the UN snapback actually does
section_path: What the UN snapback actually does
children.section_path_text: What the UN snapback actually does
children.summary: The snapback mechanism, part of UN Resolution 2231 , reinstates prior UN sanctions if Iran breaches its nuclear commitments. Triggered in August 2025 by France, Germany, and the United Kingdom, it restored restrictions on arms, finance and shipping that were originally due to expire on October 18, 2025.

children.title: Flows stay high and predictable
children.summary: Vortexa data shows Iran's crude and condensate loadings ranged between 1.3-1.6 mbd since late 2023, with several peaks above 1.8 mbd in early 2025 - the highest since 2018. Roughly 85-90% of those volumes head to China, driven by deep discounts and reported barter-style payment arrangements.

children.title: A stabilised dark fleet
children.summary: Beneath those volumes lies a network built on ship-to-ship (STS) transfers - the engine of Iran's shadow trade. Over the past three years, 82% of Iran-China voyages involved at least one STS operation, typically near Malaysia or Fujairah.

children.title: Pressure without disruption
children.summary: The next signal to watch isn't whether Iran's system can withstand pressure; it's how it adapts to it. Each new layer of enforcement, from STS monitoring to insurance tightening, is met with logistical recalibration rather than retreat.
